1→package com.grl.component.schema; 2→ 3→import java.util.List; 4→import java.util.Map; 5→import java.util.Optional; 6→import javax.annotation.Nullable; 7→import org.semanticweb.owlapi.model.OWLClass; 8→import org.semanticweb.owlapi.model.OWLIndividual; 9→import org.semanticweb.owlapi.model.OWLNamedIndividual; 10→ 11→/** 12→ * Holds detected taxonomy information for a property restriction. 13→ * 14→ *

This record captures all the information needed to generate OpenAPI schemas 15→ * for different taxonomy patterns (A, B, C, D). 16→ * 17→ * @param pattern the detected taxonomy pattern 18→ * @param individuals list of named individuals (for patterns A/B with owl:oneOf) 19→ * @param targetClass the target class for class-based taxonomies (patterns C/D) 20→ * @param hasHierarchy whether the taxonomy has a class hierarchy (pattern C) 21→ * @param conceptScheme the SKOS ConceptScheme individual (pattern D) 22→ * @param propertyName the property name this taxonomy is associated with 23→ * @param metadata individual metadata keyed by individual IRI short name (for pattern B) 24→ * @see TaxonomyPattern 25→ */ 26→public record TaxonomyInfo( 27→ TaxonomyPattern pattern, 28→ List individuals, 29→ @Nullable OWLClass targetClass, 30→ boolean hasHierarchy, 31→ @Nullable OWLIndividual conceptScheme, 32→ String propertyName, 33→ Map metadata 34→) { 35→ 36→ /** 37→ * Metadata for a typed individual (Pattern B). 38→ * 39→ * @param codeValue the code value (from codeValue annotation) 40→ * @param displayName the display name (from displayName annotation) 41→ * @param definition the definition (from skos:definition annotation) 42→ * @param domainClass the domain class type of the individual 43→ */ 44→ public record IndividualMetadata( 45→ @Nullable String codeValue, 46→ @Nullable String displayName, 47→ @Nullable String definition, 48→ @Nullable OWLClass domainClass 49→ ) { 50→ public static IndividualMetadata empty() { 51→ return new IndividualMetadata(null, null, null, null); 52→ } 53→ } 54→ 55→ /** 56→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern A (inline enum with untyped individuals). 57→ */ 58→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternA(String propertyName, List individuals) { 59→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 60→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_A, 61→ individuals, 62→ null, 63→ false, 64→ null, 65→ propertyName, 66→ Map.of() 67→ ); 68→ } 69→ 70→ /** 71→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern B (typed individuals with metadata). 72→ */ 73→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternB( 74→ String propertyName, 75→ List individuals, 76→ Map metadata) { 77→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 78→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_B, 79→ individuals, 80→ null, 81→ false, 82→ null, 83→ propertyName, 84→ metadata 85→ ); 86→ } 87→ 88→ /** 89→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern C (class-based taxonomy). 90→ */ 91→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternC( 92→ String propertyName, 93→ OWLClass targetClass, 94→ List individuals, 95→ boolean hasHierarchy) { 96→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 97→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_C, 98→ individuals, 99→ targetClass, 100→ hasHierarchy, 101→ null, 102→ propertyName, 103→ Map.of() 104→ ); 105→ } 106→ 107→ /** 108→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern D (SKOS taxonomy). 109→ */ 110→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternD( 111→ String propertyName, 112→ OWLClass targetClass, 113→ List individuals, 114→ OWLIndividual conceptScheme) { 115→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 116→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_D, 117→ individuals, 118→ targetClass, 119→ true, // SKOS hierarchies use skos:broader/narrower 120→ conceptScheme, 121→ propertyName, 122→ Map.of() 123→ ); 124→ } 125→ 126→ /** 127→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo indicating no taxonomy pattern was detected. 128→ */ 129→ public static TaxonomyInfo none(String propertyName) { 130→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 131→ TaxonomyPattern.NONE, 132→ List.of(), 133→ null, 134→ false, 135→ null, 136→ propertyName, 137→ Map.of() 138→ ); 139→ } 140→ 141→ /** 142→ * @return true if a valid taxonomy pattern was detected 143→ */ 144→ public boolean isTaxonomy() { 145→ return pattern != TaxonomyPattern.NONE; 146→ } 147→ 148→ /** 149→ * @return the target class if present 150→ */ 151→ public Optional getTargetClass() { 152→ return Optional.ofNullable(targetClass); 153→ } 154→ 155→ /** 156→ * @return the concept scheme if present (Pattern D) 157→ */ 158→ public Optional getConceptScheme() { 159→ return Optional.ofNullable(conceptScheme); 160→ } 161→} 162→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1→# Taxonomy Patterns
     2→
     3→ASG detects and maps four distinct taxonomy patterns from OWL ontologies to OpenAPI schemas. Each pattern represents a different way to model enumerated or categorized values.
     4→
     5→## Pattern Overview
     6→
     7→| Pattern | OWL Structure | OpenAPI Output | Use Case |
     8→|---------|---------------|----------------|----------|
     9→| **A** | `owl:oneOf` with untyped individuals | Inline string enum | Simple, closed enumeration |
    10→| **B** | `owl:oneOf` with typed individuals | Inline string enum | Closed enumeration with metadata |
    11→| **C** | Named class (no SKOS) | `$ref` to schema | Extensible, hierarchical taxonomy |
    12→| **D** | SKOS-based class | Inline string enum | Standards-compliant vocabulary |
    13→
    14→## Pattern Detection
    15→
    16→ASG automatically detects the appropriate pattern based on the OWL structure:
    17→
    18→```
    19→OWL Restriction
    20→├── owl:oneOf (blank node)?
    21→│   ├── Individuals have domain class? → Pattern B
    22→│   └── Untyped individuals only → Pattern A
    23→└── Named class IRI?
    24→    ├── Subclass of skos:Concept or has skos:inScheme? → Pattern D
    25→    └── No object property restrictions? → Pattern C
    26→```
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## Pattern A: Inline Enumeration (Untyped Individuals)
    31→
    32→Use Pattern A for simple, closed enumerations where you just need a list of valid values.
    33→
    34→### When to Use
    35→
    36→- Fixed set of values that rarely changes
    37→- No metadata needed per value
    38→- Values are simple labels
    39→
    40→### OWL Structure
    41→
    42→```xml
    43→<!-- Untyped individuals (only owl:NamedIndividual) -->
    44→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Active">
    45→    <rdfs:label>Active</rdfs:label>
    46→</owl:NamedIndividual>
    47→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive">
    48→    <rdfs:label>Inactive</rdfs:label>
    49→</owl:NamedIndividual>
    50→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending">
    51→    <rdfs:label>Pending</rdfs:label>
    52→</owl:NamedIndividual>
    53→
    54→<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
    55→<rdfs:subClassOf>
    56→    <owl:Restriction>
    57→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasStatus"/>
    58→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
    59→            <owl:Class>
    60→                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
    61→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Active"/>
    62→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive"/>
    63→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending"/>
    64→                </owl:oneOf>
    65→            </owl:Class>
    66→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    67→    </owl:Restriction>
    68→</rdfs:subClassOf>
    69→```
    70→
    71→### OpenAPI Output
    72→
    73→```yaml
    74→hasStatus:
    75→  type: array
    76→  minItems: 1
    77→  items:
    78→    type: string
    79→    enum:
    80→      - Active
    81→      - Inactive
    82→      - Pending
    83→```
    84→
    85→### Cardinality Variations
    86→
    87→| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Effect |
    88→|----------------|----------------|
    89→| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
    90→| `allValuesFrom` | `type: array` (optional) |
    91→| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
    92→| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array (optional) |
    93→| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |
    94→
    95→---
    96→
    97→## Pattern B: Typed Individuals with Metadata
    98→
    99→Use Pattern B when you need individuals to have a domain class and potentially carry metadata like code values, definitions, or display names.
   100→
   101→### When to Use
   102→
   103→- Values need to be typed (instances of a specific class)
   104→- Values may have metadata (codeValue, displayName, definition)
   105→- You want to query individuals via SPARQL using their type
   106→
   107→### OWL Structure
   108→
   109→```xml
   110→<!-- Domain class for the enum values -->
   111→<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode">
   112→    <skos:prefLabel>Sensor Type Code</skos:prefLabel>
   113→</owl:Class>
   114→
   115→<!-- Typed individuals with metadata -->
   116→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog">
   117→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
   118→    <skos:prefLabel>Analog</skos:prefLabel>
   119→    <skos:definition>Analog sensor type.</skos:definition>
   120→    <example:codeValue>analog</example:codeValue>
   121→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   122→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital">
   123→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
   124→    <skos:prefLabel>Digital</skos:prefLabel>
   125→    <skos:definition>Digital sensor type.</skos:definition>
   126→    <example:codeValue>digital</example:codeValue>
   127→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   128→
   129→<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
   130→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   131→    <owl:Restriction>
   132→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasSensorType"/>
   133→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   134→            <owl:Class>
   135→                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   136→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog"/>
   137→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital"/>
   138→                </owl:oneOf>
   139→            </owl:Class>
   140→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   141→    </owl:Restriction>
   142→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   143→```
   144→
   145→### OpenAPI Output
   146→
   147→```yaml
   148→hasSensorType:
   149→  type: array
   150→  minItems: 1
   151→  items:
   152→    type: string
   153→    enum:
   154→      - Analog
   155→      - Digital
   156→```
   157→
   158→### Pattern A vs B Comparison
   159→
   160→| Aspect | Pattern A | Pattern B |
   161→|--------|-----------|-----------|
   162→| Individual typing | `owl:NamedIndividual` only | Domain class + metadata |
   163→| SPARQL query | Parse `owl:oneOf` | `?x a SensorTypeCode` |
   164→| Metadata support | `rdfs:label` only | Full annotation support |
   165→| API filtering | Not supported | Via `apiLabel` on individuals |
   166→
   167→---
   168→
   169→## Pattern C: Class-Based Taxonomy (Extensible)
   170→
   171→Use Pattern C for extensible taxonomies where values can be added without modifying the restriction, and optionally support hierarchical structure.
   172→
   173→### When to Use
   174→
   175→- Values should be discoverable via class membership
   176→- New values can be added without changing the schema
   177→- Hierarchical grouping is needed (via subclasses)
   178→- You want a reusable schema in components/schemas
   179→
   180→### OWL Structure
   181→
   182→```xml
   183→<!-- Parent taxonomy class -->
   184→<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode">
   185→    <skos:prefLabel>Environment Code</skos:prefLabel>
   186→    <skos:definition>Operating environment classification.</skos:definition>
   187→</owl:Class>
   188→
   189→<!-- Subclasses for hierarchy (optional) -->
   190→<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment">
   191→    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
   192→    <skos:prefLabel>Indoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
   193→</owl:Class>
   194→<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment">
   195→    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
   196→    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
   197→</owl:Class>
   198→
   199→<!-- Individuals (instances of subclasses) -->
   200→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOffice">
   201→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment"/>
   202→    <skos:prefLabel>Office</skos:prefLabel>
   203→    <example:codeValue>office</example:codeValue>
   204→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   205→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOutdoor">
   206→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment"/>
   207→    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor</skos:prefLabel>
   208→    <example:codeValue>outdoor</example:codeValue>
   209→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   210→
   211→<!-- Restriction to named class (NOT owl:oneOf) -->
   212→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   213→    <owl:Restriction>
   214→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasEnvironment"/>
   215→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
   216→    </owl:Restriction>
   217→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   218→```
   219→
   220→### OpenAPI Output
   221→
   222→```yaml
   223→# On the resource
   224→hasEnvironment:
   225→  type: array
   226→  minItems: 1
   227→  items:
   228→    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EnvironmentCode'
   229→
   230→# In components/schemas
   231→EnvironmentCode:
   232→  type: object
   233→  properties:
   234→    code:
   235→      type: string
   236→      enum:
   237→        - office
   238→        - outdoor
   239→    displayName:
   240→      type: string
   241→```
   242→
   243→### Flat vs Hierarchical
   244→
   245→Pattern C supports both flat taxonomies (direct instances of the class) and hierarchical taxonomies (instances of subclasses).
   246→
   247→**Flat Taxonomy:**
   248→```
   249→EnvironmentCode
   250→├── EnvOffice (instance)
   251→├── EnvWarehouse (instance)
   252→└── EnvOutdoor (instance)
   253→```
   254→
   255→**Hierarchical Taxonomy:**
   256→```
   257→EnvironmentCode
   258→├── IndoorEnvironment (subclass)
   259→│   ├── EnvOffice (instance)
   260→│   └── EnvWarehouse (instance)
   261→└── OutdoorEnvironment (subclass)
   262→    └── EnvOutdoor (instance)
   263→```
   264→
   265→### Pattern B vs C Comparison
   266→
   267→| Aspect | Pattern B | Pattern C |
   268→|--------|-----------|-----------|
   269→| Restriction target | Blank node with `owl:oneOf` | Named class IRI |
   270→| Set closure | Closed (explicit list) | Open (query instances) |
   271→| Adding values | Requires changing `owl:oneOf` | Just add instance |
   272→| Hierarchy | Flat only | Supports subclass hierarchy |
   273→| OpenAPI output | Inline enum | `$ref` to schema |
   274→
   275→---
   276→
   277→## Pattern D: SKOS Taxonomy
   278→
   279→Use Pattern D for standards-compliant vocabularies using SKOS (Simple Knowledge Organization System), which enables interoperability with terminology servers and FHIR-compatible systems.
   280→
   281→### When to Use
   282→
   283→- Standards compliance required (SKOS, FHIR)
   284→- Multi-language support needed (`skos:prefLabel` with `@lang`)
   285→- Hierarchical relationships (`skos:broader`, `skos:narrower`)
   286→- External code mapping (`skos:exactMatch`)
   287→- Terminology server integration
   288→
   289→### OWL Structure
   290→
   291→```xml
   292→<!-- SKOS ConceptScheme -->
   293→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme">
   294→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#ConceptScheme"/>
   295→    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code System</skos:prefLabel>
   296→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   297→
   298→<!-- Bridge class linking to SKOS scheme -->
   299→<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode">
   300→    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   301→    <owl:equivalentClass>
   302→        <owl:Restriction>
   303→            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#inScheme"/>
   304→            <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   305→        </owl:Restriction>
   306→    </owl:equivalentClass>
   307→    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code</skos:prefLabel>
   308→</owl:Class>
   309→
   310→<!-- SKOS Concepts -->
   311→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextContinuous">
   312→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   313→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   314→    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   315→    <skos:notation>continuous</skos:notation>
   316→    <skos:prefLabel>Continuous</skos:prefLabel>
   317→    <skos:definition>Continuous measurement context.</skos:definition>
   318→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   319→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextDiscrete">
   320→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   321→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   322→    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   323→    <skos:notation>discrete</skos:notation>
   324→    <skos:prefLabel>Discrete</skos:prefLabel>
   325→    <skos:definition>Discrete measurement context.</skos:definition>
   326→    <skos:narrower rdf:resource="https://example.com/ContextTriggered"/>
   327→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   328→
   329→<!-- Restriction to SKOS-based class -->
   330→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   331→    <owl:Restriction>
   332→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasMeasurementContext"/>
   333→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   334→    </owl:Restriction>
   335→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   336→```
   337→
   338→### OpenAPI Output
   339→
   340→```yaml
   341→MeasurementContextCode:
   342→  type: array
   343→  minItems: 1
   344→  items:
   345→    type: string
   346→    enum:
   347→      - Continuous
   348→      - Discrete
   349→      - Triggered
   350→```
   351→
   352→### SKOS Detection Criteria
   353→
   354→ASG detects Pattern D when the target class:
   355→- Is `rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept`, OR
   356→- Has an `owl:equivalentClass` with a `skos:inScheme` restriction
   357→
   358→### Pattern C vs D Comparison
   359→
   360→| Aspect | Pattern C (Class-based) | Pattern D (SKOS) |
   361→|--------|-------------------------|------------------|
   362→| Standard | OWL only | OWL + SKOS |
   363→| Hierarchy | `rdfs:subClassOf` | `skos:broader/narrower` |
   364→| Membership | `rdf:type` | `skos:inScheme` |
   365→| Multi-language | Not native | `skos:prefLabel@en` |
   366→| External mapping | Not native | `skos:exactMatch` |
   367→| Discovery | `?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* Class` | `?x skos:inScheme Scheme` |
   368→
   369→---
   370→
   371→## Cardinality Summary
   372→
   373→All patterns support the same cardinality restrictions:
   374→
   375→| OWL Restriction | Required? | Array? | minItems | maxItems |
   376→|-----------------|-----------|--------|----------|----------|
   377→| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Yes | No | - | - |
   378→| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | No | No | - | - |
   379→| `someValuesFrom` | Yes | Yes | 1 | - |
   380→| `allValuesFrom` | No | Yes | - | - |
   381→| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | Yes (if n≥1) | Yes | n | - |
   382→| `maxQualifiedCardinality(n)` | No | Yes | - | n |
   383→
   384→---
   385→
   386→## Choosing the Right Pattern
   387→
   388→| Requirement | Recommended Pattern |
   389→|-------------|---------------------|
   390→| Simple closed list, rarely changes | Pattern A |
   391→| Typed values with metadata | Pattern B |
   392→| Extensible, add values without schema change | Pattern C |
   393→| Hierarchical grouping needed | Pattern C or D |
   394→| Standards compliance (SKOS, FHIR) | Pattern D |
   395→| Multi-language support | Pattern D |
   396→| External terminology mapping | Pattern D |
   397→

# Taxonomy Patterns

ASG detects and maps four distinct taxonomy patterns from OWL ontologies to OpenAPI schemas. Each pattern represents a different way to model enumerated or categorized values.

## Pattern Overview

| Pattern | OWL Structure | OpenAPI Output | Use Case |
|---------|---------------|----------------|----------|
| **A** | `owl:oneOf` with untyped individuals | Inline string enum | Simple, closed enumeration |
| **B** | `owl:oneOf` with typed individuals | Inline string enum | Closed enumeration with metadata |
| **C** | Named class (no SKOS) | `$ref` to schema | Extensible, hierarchical taxonomy |
| **D** | SKOS-based class | Inline string enum | Standards-compliant vocabulary |

## Pattern Detection

ASG automatically detects the appropriate pattern based on the OWL structure:

```
OWL Restriction
├── owl:oneOf (blank node)?
│   ├── Individuals have domain class? → Pattern B
│   └── Untyped individuals only → Pattern A
└── Named class IRI?
    ├── Subclass of skos:Concept or has skos:inScheme? → Pattern D
    └── No object property restrictions? → Pattern C
```

---

## Pattern A: Inline Enumeration (Untyped Individuals)

Use Pattern A for simple, closed enumerations where you just need a list of valid values.

### When to Use

- Fixed set of values that rarely changes
- No metadata needed per value
- Values are simple labels

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Untyped individuals (only owl:NamedIndividual) -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Active">
    <rdfs:label>Active</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive">
    <rdfs:label>Inactive</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending">
    <rdfs:label>Pending</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasStatus"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Active"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
hasStatus:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Active
      - Inactive
      - Pending
```

### Cardinality Variations

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Effect |
|----------------|----------------|
| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
| `allValuesFrom` | `type: array` (optional) |
| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array (optional) |
| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |

---

## Pattern B: Typed Individuals with Metadata

Use Pattern B when you need individuals to have a domain class and potentially carry metadata like code values, definitions, or display names.

### When to Use

- Values need to be typed (instances of a specific class)
- Values may have metadata (codeValue, displayName, definition)
- You want to query individuals via SPARQL using their type

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Domain class for the enum values -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode">
    <skos:prefLabel>Sensor Type Code</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Typed individuals with metadata -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Analog</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Analog sensor type.</skos:definition>
    <example:codeValue>analog</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Digital</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Digital sensor type.</skos:definition>
    <example:codeValue>digital</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasSensorType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
hasSensorType:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Analog
      - Digital
```

### Pattern A vs B Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern A | Pattern B |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Individual typing | `owl:NamedIndividual` only | Domain class + metadata |
| SPARQL query | Parse `owl:oneOf` | `?x a SensorTypeCode` |
| Metadata support | `rdfs:label` only | Full annotation support |
| API filtering | Not supported | Via `apiLabel` on individuals |

---

## Pattern C: Class-Based Taxonomy (Extensible)

Use Pattern C for extensible taxonomies where values can be added without modifying the restriction, and optionally support hierarchical structure.

### When to Use

- Values should be discoverable via class membership
- New values can be added without changing the schema
- Hierarchical grouping is needed (via subclasses)
- You want a reusable schema in components/schemas

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Parent taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode">
    <skos:prefLabel>Environment Code</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Operating environment classification.</skos:definition>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Subclasses for hierarchy (optional) -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Indoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Individuals (instances of subclasses) -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOffice">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Office</skos:prefLabel>
    <example:codeValue>office</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOutdoor">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor</skos:prefLabel>
    <example:codeValue>outdoor</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction to named class (NOT owl:oneOf) -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasEnvironment"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
# On the resource
hasEnvironment:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EnvironmentCode'

# In components/schemas
EnvironmentCode:
  type: object
  properties:
    code:
      type: string
      enum:
        - office
        - outdoor
    displayName:
      type: string
```

### Flat vs Hierarchical

Pattern C supports both flat taxonomies (direct instances of the class) and hierarchical taxonomies (instances of subclasses).

**Flat Taxonomy:**
```
EnvironmentCode
├── EnvOffice (instance)
├── EnvWarehouse (instance)
└── EnvOutdoor (instance)
```

**Hierarchical Taxonomy:**
```
EnvironmentCode
├── IndoorEnvironment (subclass)
│   ├── EnvOffice (instance)
│   └── EnvWarehouse (instance)
└── OutdoorEnvironment (subclass)
    └── EnvOutdoor (instance)
```

### Pattern B vs C Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern B | Pattern C |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Restriction target | Blank node with `owl:oneOf` | Named class IRI |
| Set closure | Closed (explicit list) | Open (query instances) |
| Adding values | Requires changing `owl:oneOf` | Just add instance |
| Hierarchy | Flat only | Supports subclass hierarchy |
| OpenAPI output | Inline enum | `$ref` to schema |

---

## Pattern D: SKOS Taxonomy

Use Pattern D for standards-compliant vocabularies using SKOS (Simple Knowledge Organization System), which enables interoperability with terminology servers and FHIR-compatible systems.

### When to Use

- Standards compliance required (SKOS, FHIR)
- Multi-language support needed (`skos:prefLabel` with `@lang`)
- Hierarchical relationships (`skos:broader`, `skos:narrower`)
- External code mapping (`skos:exactMatch`)
- Terminology server integration

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- SKOS ConceptScheme -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#ConceptScheme"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code System</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Bridge class linking to SKOS scheme -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <owl:equivalentClass>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#inScheme"/>
            <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </owl:equivalentClass>
    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- SKOS Concepts -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextContinuous">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
    <skos:notation>continuous</skos:notation>
    <skos:prefLabel>Continuous</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Continuous measurement context.</skos:definition>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextDiscrete">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
    <skos:notation>discrete</skos:notation>
    <skos:prefLabel>Discrete</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Discrete measurement context.</skos:definition>
    <skos:narrower rdf:resource="https://example.com/ContextTriggered"/>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction to SKOS-based class -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasMeasurementContext"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
MeasurementContextCode:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Continuous
      - Discrete
      - Triggered
```

### SKOS Detection Criteria

ASG detects Pattern D when the target class:
- Is `rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept`, OR
- Has an `owl:equivalentClass` with a `skos:inScheme` restriction

### Pattern C vs D Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern C (Class-based) | Pattern D (SKOS) |
|--------|-------------------------|------------------|
| Standard | OWL only | OWL + SKOS |
| Hierarchy | `rdfs:subClassOf` | `skos:broader/narrower` |
| Membership | `rdf:type` | `skos:inScheme` |
| Multi-language | Not native | `skos:prefLabel@en` |
| External mapping | Not native | `skos:exactMatch` |
| Discovery | `?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* Class` | `?x skos:inScheme Scheme` |

---

## Cardinality Summary

All patterns support the same cardinality restrictions:

| OWL Restriction | Required? | Array? | minItems | maxItems |
|-----------------|-----------|--------|----------|----------|
| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Yes | No | - | - |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | No | No | - | - |
| `someValuesFrom` | Yes | Yes | 1 | - |
| `allValuesFrom` | No | Yes | - | - |
| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | Yes (if n≥1) | Yes | n | - |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(n)` | No | Yes | - | n |

---

## Choosing the Right Pattern

| Requirement | Recommended Pattern |
|-------------|---------------------|
| Simple closed list, rarely changes | Pattern A |
| Typed values with metadata | Pattern B |
| Extensible, add values without schema change | Pattern C |
| Hierarchical grouping needed | Pattern C or D |
| Standards compliance (SKOS, FHIR) | Pattern D |
| Multi-language support | Pattern D |
| External terminology mapping | Pattern D |

   102→enforceRequiredness: true

   119→
   120→**As a map** (explicit target namespace):
   121→
   122→```yaml
   123→namespaceMappings:

   128→
   129→Patterns prefixed with `!` are exclusions.
   130→
   131→### `securitySchemeName`
   132→
   133→**Type:** `string`
   134→
   135→Name of the security scheme to use. If not specified, the first OAuth2 scheme found in the OpenAPI template is used.
   136→
   137→```yaml
   138→securitySchemeName: oauth2
   139→```
   140→
   141→Supports placeholders: `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}`, `{security}`
   142→
   143→### `defaultSecurityTemplate`
   144→
   145→**Type:** `string`
   146→
   147→Template for generating security requirements when no `security` annotation is present on a resource.
   148→
   149→```yaml
   150→defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}:{model}:{action}"

   152→

   154→- `{api}` - The API name from config
   155→- `{action}` - HTTP method mapped via `securityActionMappings`
   156→- `{model}` - The resource name
   157→

   159→
   160→### `securityTemplate`
   161→
   162→**Type:** `string`
   163→
   164→Template for generating security requirements when a `security` annotation IS present.
   165→
   166→```yaml
   167→securityTemplate: "{security}"
   168→```
   169→
   170→**Placeholders:**
   171→- `{security}` - The value from the `security` annotation
   172→- `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}` - Same as above
   173→
   174→### `securityActionMappings`
   175→
   176→**Type:** `map`
   177→
   178→Maps HTTP methods to action names for use in security templates.
   179→
   180→```yaml
   181→securityActionMappings:
   182→  GET: read
   183→  POST: create
   184→  PUT: update
   185→  PATCH: update
   186→  DELETE: delete
   187→```
   188→
   189→### `templates`
   190→
   191→**Type:** `object`
   192→
   193→OpenAPI template objects. See [Templates](templates.md) for details.
   194→
   195→```yaml
   196→templates:
   197→  openapi:
   198→    openapi: 3.1.0
   199→    info:
   200→      title: My API
   201→      version: 1.0.0
   202→  collectionPathItem:
   203→    # PathItem template for collection endpoints
   204→  singlePathItem:
   205→    # PathItem template for single-resource endpoints
   206→```
   207→
   208→## Complete Example
   209→
   210→```yaml
   211→extends: base-config.yaml
   212→
   213→name: employment_api
   214→
   215→ontologies:
   216→  - ${configDir}/ontologies/employment.owl
   217→  - ${configDir}/ontologies/extensions.owl
   218→
   219→outputDir: ${configDir}/../../output
   220→
   221→catalogFile: ${configDir}/ontologies/catalog.xml
   222→
   223→rootClass: https://example.com/employment/EmploymentAPIV1.0
   224→
   225→enforceRequiredness: true
   226→
   227→namespaceMappings:
   228→  https://example.com/employment/:
   229→    - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
   230→    - ".*"
   231→
   232→securitySchemeName: oauth2
   233→defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
   234→securityActionMappings:
   235→  GET: read
   236→  POST: create
   237→  PUT: update
   238→  PATCH: update
   239→  DELETE: delete
   240→
   241→templates:
   242→  openapi:
   243→    openapi: 3.1.0
   244→    info:
   245→      title: Employment API
   246→      version: 1.0.0
   247→      description: HR management API
   248→      contact:
   249→        name: API Support
   250→        email: support@example.com
   251→    servers:
   252→      - url: https://api.example.com/v1
   253→        description: Production
   254→    components:
   255→      securitySchemes:
   256→        oauth2:
   257→          type: oauth2
   258→          flows:
   259→            authorizationCode:
   260→              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
   261→              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
   262→```

file.content:
# Configuration Reference

Complete reference for all YAML configuration options.

## Required Fields

### `name`

**Type:** `string` (alphanumeric and underscores only)

The name of the API being generated. Used as:
- Output subdirectory name
- Namespace identifier
- Part of operation IDs

```yaml
name: myapi
```

### `ontologies`

**Type:** `list of URI/path strings`

List of ontology files to load. Multiple files are merged into a single ontology.

```yaml
ontologies:
  - ./my-ontology.owl
  - ./extensions.owl
```

Supports:
- Relative paths (resolved from config file location)
- Absolute paths
- URIs (`file://`, `http://`, `https://`)

### `outputDir`

**Type:** `path`

Directory where generated files will be written. A subdirectory named after `name` will be created.

```yaml
outputDir: ./output
```

Output structure:
```
output/myapi/
├── effective-config.yaml
├── ontology0.owl
├── ontology-flat.owl
├── ontology-merged.owl
├── openapi.json
└── openapi.yaml
```

## Optional Fields

### `extends`

**Type:** `path`

Path to another configuration file to extend. Fields are merged recursively.

```yaml
extends: ../base-config.yaml
```

See [Configuration Overview](index.md) for merge behavior details.

### `rootClass`

**Type:** `IRI string`

The IRI of the root class that defines the API resources. Required if multiple classes are subclasses of `APIRoot`.

```yaml
rootClass: https://example.com/ontology/MyAPIV1.0
```

If not specified, ASG infers the root from the single class that is a subclass of `APIRoot`.

### `catalogFile`

**Type:** `path`

Path to an XML catalog file for resolving ontology imports.

```yaml
catalogFile: ./catalog-v001.xml
```

### `enforceRequiredness`

**Type:** `boolean`
**Default:** `false`

When `true`, properties with cardinality restrictions (`minCardinality >= 1`, `exactCardinality >= 1`, or `someValuesFrom`) are added to the `required` array in the JSON Schema.

```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
```

### `namespaceMappings`

**Type:** `map` or `list`

Defines how IRIs are flattened into a single namespace. Values are regex patterns to match source namespaces.

**As a list** (target namespace inferred from first ontology):

```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#.*"     # Exclude OWL namespace
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#.*"  # Exclude XSD namespace
  - ".*"                                      # Include everything else
```

**As a map** (explicit target namespace):

```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/api/:
    - https://example.com/.*
    - https://other.com/.*
```

Patterns prefixed with `!` are exclusions.

### `securitySchemeName`

**Type:** `string`

Name of the security scheme to use. If not specified, the first OAuth2 scheme found in the OpenAPI template is used.

```yaml
securitySchemeName: oauth2
```

Supports placeholders: `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}`, `{security}`

### `defaultSecurityTemplate`

**Type:** `string`

Template for generating security requirements when no `security` annotation is present on a resource.

```yaml
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}:{model}:{action}"
```

**Placeholders:**
- `{api}` - The API name from config
- `{action}` - HTTP method mapped via `securityActionMappings`
- `{model}` - The resource name

Set to `null` or empty to suppress security generation for unannotated resources.

### `securityTemplate`

**Type:** `string`

Template for generating security requirements when a `security` annotation IS present.

```yaml
securityTemplate: "{security}"
```

**Placeholders:**
- `{security}` - The value from the `security` annotation
- `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}` - Same as above

### `securityActionMappings`

**Type:** `map`

Maps HTTP methods to action names for use in security templates.

```yaml
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete
```

### `templates`

**Type:** `object`

OpenAPI template objects. See [Templates](templates.md) for details.

```yaml
templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
    info:
      title: My API
      version: 1.0.0
  collectionPathItem:
    # PathItem template for collection endpoints
  singlePathItem:
    # PathItem template for single-resource endpoints
```

## Complete Example

```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml

name: employment_api

ontologies:
  - ${configDir}/ontologies/employment.owl
  - ${configDir}/ontologies/extensions.owl

outputDir: ${configDir}/../../output

catalogFile: ${configDir}/ontologies/catalog.xml

rootClass: https://example.com/employment/EmploymentAPIV1.0

enforceRequiredness: true

namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/employment/:
    - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
    - ".*"

securitySchemeName: oauth2
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete

templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
    info:
      title: Employment API
      version: 1.0.0
      description: HR management API
      contact:
        name: API Support
        email: support@example.com
    servers:
      - url: https://api.example.com/v1
        description: Production
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        oauth2:
          type: oauth2
          flows:
            authorizationCode:
              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
```

   183→        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   184→        <skos:notation>continuous</skos:notation>
   185→        <skos:prefLabel>Continuous</skos:prefLabel>

   187→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   188→

   197→

   199→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   200→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   201→        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>

   203→        <skos:prefLabel>Triggered</skos:prefLabel>

   206→    </owl:NamedIndividual>

   209→        <skos:prefLabel>Has Measurement Context</skos:prefLabel>

   211→    </owl:ObjectProperty>

   215→    <!-- ================================================================== -->

   217→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource">
   218→        <skos:prefLabel>Pattern Test Resource</skos:prefLabel>
   219→        <skos:definition>Resource class to test all taxonomy patterns.</skos:definition>

   221→        <!-- apiID -->

   224→        <!-- Pattern A: owl:oneOf with untyped individuals -->
   225→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr"/>
   226→
   227→        <!-- Pattern B: owl:oneOf with typed individuals -->
   228→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr"/>
   229→
   230→        <!-- Pattern C: someValuesFrom named class (extensible) -->
   231→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr"/>
   232→
   233→        <!-- Pattern D: someValuesFrom SKOS-based class -->
   234→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr"/>
   235→    </owl:Class>
   236→
   237→    <!-- apiID restriction -->
   238→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="patternApiId">
   239→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/apiID"/>
   240→        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
   241→        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
   242→    </owl:Restriction>
   243→    <owl:Axiom>
   244→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   245→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   246→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="patternApiId"/>
   247→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   248→    </owl:Axiom>
   249→
   250→    <!-- Pattern A: hasStatusA restriction with owl:oneOf (untyped individuals) -->
   251→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr">
   252→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasStatusA"/>
   253→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   254→            <owl:Class>
   255→                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   256→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Active"/>
   257→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Inactive"/>
   258→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Pending"/>
   259→                </owl:oneOf>
   260→            </owl:Class>
   261→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   262→    </owl:Restriction>
   263→    <owl:Axiom>
   264→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   265→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   266→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr"/>
   267→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   268→    </owl:Axiom>
   269→
   270→    <!-- Pattern B: hasSensorType restriction with owl:oneOf (typed individuals) -->
   271→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr">
   272→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasSensorType"/>
   273→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   274→            <owl:Class>
   275→                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   276→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeAnalog"/>
   277→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeDigital"/>
   278→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeHybrid"/>
   279→                </owl:oneOf>
   280→            </owl:Class>
   281→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   282→    </owl:Restriction>
   283→    <owl:Axiom>
   284→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   285→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   286→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr"/>
   287→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   288→    </owl:Axiom>
   289→
   290→    <!-- Pattern C: hasEnvironment restriction (someValuesFrom named class) -->
   291→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr">
   292→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasEnvironment"/>
   293→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode"/>
   294→    </owl:Restriction>
   295→    <owl:Axiom>
   296→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   297→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   298→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr"/>
   299→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   300→    </owl:Axiom>
   301→
   302→    <!-- Pattern D: hasMeasurementContext restriction (someValuesFrom SKOS class) -->
   303→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr">
   304→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasMeasurementContext"/>
   305→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   306→    </owl:Restriction>
   307→    <owl:Axiom>
   308→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   309→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   310→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr"/>
   311→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   312→    </owl:Axiom>
   313→
   314→</rdf:RDF>

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xmlns="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/"
     xml:base="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/"
     xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
     xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
     xmlns:xml="http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
     xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#"
     xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
     xmlns:skos="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#"
     xmlns:base="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/"
     xmlns:api="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/"
     xmlns:tp="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/">

    <owl:Ontology rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/">
        <owl:imports rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/V1.0.2"/>
        <rdfs:comment>Test ontology for taxonomy pattern detection (Patterns A, B, C, D).</rdfs:comment>
    </owl:Ontology>

    <!-- APIRoot links to API specification -->
    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/APIRoot">
        <rdfs:subClassOf>
            <owl:Restriction>
                <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasAPI"/>
                <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0"/>
            </owl:Restriction>
        </rdfs:subClassOf>
    </rdf:Description>

    <!-- API Specification Class -->
    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0">
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/GRLAPISpecifications"/>
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasPatternTestResource"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Taxonomy Patterns API V1.0</skos:prefLabel>
        <api:apiDisplayLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiDisplayLabel>
    </owl:Class>

    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasPatternTestResource">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasResourceSchema"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onClass rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasPatternTestResource"/>
        <api:apitag>PatternTestResource</api:apitag>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    <!-- PATTERN A: Inline enumeration with untyped individuals             -->
    <!-- ================================================================== -->

    <!-- Pattern A - Untyped individuals (only owl:NamedIndividual) -->
    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Active">
        <rdfs:label>Active</rdfs:label>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Inactive">
        <rdfs:label>Inactive</rdfs:label>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Pending">
        <rdfs:label>Pending</rdfs:label>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasStatusA">
        <skos:prefLabel>Has Status A</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Pattern A: Status using untyped individuals.</skos:definition>
    </owl:ObjectProperty>

    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    <!-- PATTERN B: Typed individuals with metadata                         -->
    <!-- ================================================================== -->

    <!-- Pattern B - Domain class for typed individuals -->
    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode">
        <skos:prefLabel>Sensor Type Code</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Code values for sensor types.</skos:definition>
    </owl:Class>

    <!-- Pattern B - Typed individuals with codeValue -->
    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeAnalog">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Analog</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Analog sensor type.</skos:definition>
        <tp:codeValue>analog</tp:codeValue>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeDigital">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Digital</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Digital sensor type.</skos:definition>
        <tp:codeValue>digital</tp:codeValue>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeHybrid">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Hybrid</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Hybrid sensor type.</skos:definition>
        <tp:codeValue>hybrid</tp:codeValue>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:AnnotationProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/codeValue"/>

    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasSensorType">
        <skos:prefLabel>Has Sensor Type</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Pattern B: Sensor type using typed individuals.</skos:definition>
    </owl:ObjectProperty>

    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    <!-- PATTERN C: Class-based taxonomy (extensible)                       -->
    <!-- ================================================================== -->

    <!-- Pattern C - Taxonomy class -->
    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode">
        <skos:prefLabel>Environment Code</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Operating environment classification.</skos:definition>
    </owl:Class>

    <!-- Pattern C - Subclasses for hierarchy -->
    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/IndoorEnvironment">
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Indoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
    </owl:Class>

    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/OutdoorEnvironment">
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
    </owl:Class>

    <!-- Pattern C - Individuals -->
    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvOffice">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/IndoorEnvironment"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Office</skos:prefLabel>
        <tp:codeValue>office</tp:codeValue>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvWarehouse">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/IndoorEnvironment"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Warehouse</skos:prefLabel>
        <tp:codeValue>warehouse</tp:codeValue>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvOutdoor">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/OutdoorEnvironment"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor</skos:prefLabel>
        <tp:codeValue>outdoor</tp:codeValue>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasEnvironment">
        <skos:prefLabel>Has Environment</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Pattern C: Environment using class-based taxonomy.</skos:definition>
    </owl:ObjectProperty>

    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    <!-- PATTERN D: SKOS taxonomy                                           -->
    <!-- ================================================================== -->

    <!-- Pattern D - SKOS ConceptScheme -->
    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#ConceptScheme"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code System</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Code system for measurement context types.</skos:definition>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <!-- Pattern D - Bridge class linking to SKOS scheme -->
    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode">
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
        <owl:equivalentClass>
            <owl:Restriction>
                <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#inScheme"/>
                <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
            </owl:Restriction>
        </owl:equivalentClass>
        <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>SKOS-based measurement context codes.</skos:definition>
    </owl:Class>

    <!-- Pattern D - SKOS Concepts -->
    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextContinuous">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
        <skos:notation>continuous</skos:notation>
        <skos:prefLabel>Continuous</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Continuous measurement context.</skos:definition>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextDiscrete">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
        <skos:notation>discrete</skos:notation>
        <skos:prefLabel>Discrete</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Discrete measurement context.</skos:definition>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextTriggered">
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
        <skos:notation>triggered</skos:notation>
        <skos:prefLabel>Triggered</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Event-triggered measurement context.</skos:definition>
        <skos:broader rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextDiscrete"/>
    </owl:NamedIndividual>

    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasMeasurementContext">
        <skos:prefLabel>Has Measurement Context</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Pattern D: Measurement context using SKOS taxonomy.</skos:definition>
    </owl:ObjectProperty>

    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    <!-- MAIN RESOURCE CLASS                                                -->
    <!-- ================================================================== -->

    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource">
        <skos:prefLabel>Pattern Test Resource</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Resource class to test all taxonomy patterns.</skos:definition>

        <!-- apiID -->
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="patternApiId"/>

        <!-- Pattern A: owl:oneOf with untyped individuals -->
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr"/>

        <!-- Pattern B: owl:oneOf with typed individuals -->
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr"/>

        <!-- Pattern C: someValuesFrom named class (extensible) -->
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr"/>

        <!-- Pattern D: someValuesFrom SKOS-based class -->
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr"/>
    </owl:Class>

    <!-- apiID restriction -->
    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="patternApiId">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/apiID"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="patternApiId"/>
        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Pattern A: hasStatusA restriction with owl:oneOf (untyped individuals) -->
    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasStatusA"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Active"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Inactive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Pending"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr"/>
        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Pattern B: hasSensorType restriction with owl:oneOf (typed individuals) -->
    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasSensorType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeAnalog"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeDigital"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeHybrid"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr"/>
        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Pattern C: hasEnvironment restriction (someValuesFrom named class) -->
    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasEnvironment"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr"/>
        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Pattern D: hasMeasurementContext restriction (someValuesFrom SKOS class) -->
    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasMeasurementContext"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr"/>
        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>

</rdf:RDF>

input.new_string:
### `enforceRequiredness`

**Type:** `boolean`
**Default:** `false`

When `true`, properties with cardinality restrictions (`minCardinality >= 1`, `exactCardinality >= 1`, or `someValuesFrom`) are added to the `required` array in the JSON Schema.

```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
```

### `maxIndividualDepth`

**Type:** `integer`
**Default:** `2`

Controls how deeply object property assertions on Pattern B taxonomy individuals are expanded when generating `oneOf` schemas with const objects.

```yaml
maxIndividualDepth: 2
```

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `0` | Legacy mode: generates simple string enum (backward compatible) |
| `1` | Single level: only direct property assertions on individuals |
| `2+` | Recursive: expands nested individuals up to specified depth |

**Example with depth 2:**

OWL individual with nested object property:
```turtle
sensor:SensorTypeAnalog a sensor:SensorTypeCode ;
    skos:notation "analog" ;
    sensor:hasCategory sensor:MeasurementCategory .

sensor:MeasurementCategory a owl:NamedIndividual ;
    skos:notation "measurement" ;
    sensor:unit "volts" .
```

Generates `oneOf` with nested objects:
```yaml
oneOf:
  - type: object
    properties:
      code:
        const: "analog"
        type: string
      hasCategory:
        type: object
        properties:
          code:
            const: "measurement"
            type: string
          unit:
            const: "volts"
            type: string
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Configuration Reference

Complete reference for all YAML configuration options.

## Required Fields

### `name`

**Type:** `string` (alphanumeric and underscores only)

The name of the API being generated. Used as:
- Output subdirectory name
- Namespace identifier
- Part of operation IDs

```yaml
name: myapi
```

### `ontologies`

**Type:** `list of URI/path strings`

List of ontology files to load. Multiple files are merged into a single ontology.

```yaml
ontologies:
  - ./my-ontology.owl
  - ./extensions.owl
```

Supports:
- Relative paths (resolved from config file location)
- Absolute paths
- URIs (`file://`, `http://`, `https://`)

### `outputDir`

**Type:** `path`

Directory where generated files will be written. A subdirectory named after `name` will be created.

```yaml
outputDir: ./output
```

Output structure:
```
output/myapi/
├── effective-config.yaml
├── ontology0.owl
├── ontology-flat.owl
├── ontology-merged.owl
├── openapi.json
└── openapi.yaml
```

## Optional Fields

### `extends`

**Type:** `path`

Path to another configuration file to extend. Fields are merged recursively.

```yaml
extends: ../base-config.yaml
```

See [Configuration Overview](index.md) for merge behavior details.

### `rootClass`

**Type:** `IRI string`

The IRI of the root class that defines the API resources. Required if multiple classes are subclasses of `APIRoot`.

```yaml
rootClass: https://example.com/ontology/MyAPIV1.0
```

If not specified, ASG infers the root from the single class that is a subclass of `APIRoot`.

### `catalogFile`

**Type:** `path`

Path to an XML catalog file for resolving ontology imports.

```yaml
catalogFile: ./catalog-v001.xml
```

### `enforceRequiredness`

**Type:** `boolean`
**Default:** `false`

When `true`, properties with cardinality restrictions (`minCardinality >= 1`, `exactCardinality >= 1`, or `someValuesFrom`) are added to the `required` array in the JSON Schema.

```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
```

### `namespaceMappings`

**Type:** `map` or `list`

Defines how IRIs are flattened into a single namespace. Values are regex patterns to match source namespaces.

**As a list** (target namespace inferred from first ontology):

```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#.*"     # Exclude OWL namespace
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#.*"  # Exclude XSD namespace
  - ".*"                                      # Include everything else
```

**As a map** (explicit target namespace):

```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/api/:
    - https://example.com/.*
    - https://other.com/.*
```

Patterns prefixed with `!` are exclusions.

### `securitySchemeName`

**Type:** `string`

Name of the security scheme to use. If not specified, the first OAuth2 scheme found in the OpenAPI template is used.

```yaml
securitySchemeName: oauth2
```

Supports placeholders: `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}`, `{security}`

### `defaultSecurityTemplate`

**Type:** `string`

Template for generating security requirements when no `security` annotation is present on a resource.

```yaml
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}:{model}:{action}"
```

**Placeholders:**
- `{api}` - The API name from config
- `{action}` - HTTP method mapped via `securityActionMappings`
- `{model}` - The resource name

Set to `null` or empty to suppress security generation for unannotated resources.

### `securityTemplate`

**Type:** `string`

Template for generating security requirements when a `security` annotation IS present.

```yaml
securityTemplate: "{security}"
```

**Placeholders:**
- `{security}` - The value from the `security` annotation
- `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}` - Same as above

### `securityActionMappings`

**Type:** `map`

Maps HTTP methods to action names for use in security templates.

```yaml
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete
```

### `templates`

**Type:** `object`

OpenAPI template objects. See [Templates](templates.md) for details.

```yaml
templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
    info:
      title: My API
      version: 1.0.0
  collectionPathItem:
    # PathItem template for collection endpoints
  singlePathItem:
    # PathItem template for single-resource endpoints
```

## Complete Example

```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml

name: employment_api

ontologies:
  - ${configDir}/ontologies/employment.owl
  - ${configDir}/ontologies/extensions.owl

outputDir: ${configDir}/../../output

catalogFile: ${configDir}/ontologies/catalog.xml

rootClass: https://example.com/employment/EmploymentAPIV1.0

enforceRequiredness: true

namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/employment/:
    - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
    - ".*"

securitySchemeName: oauth2
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete

templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
    info:
      title: Employment API
      version: 1.0.0
      description: HR management API
      contact:
        name: API Support
        email: support@example.com
    servers:
      - url: https://api.example.com/v1
        description: Production
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        oauth2:
          type: oauth2
          flows:
            authorizationCode:
              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
```
